title: Enable PDF download with formatting matching uploaded research paper template

requirements.summary: Replace text file download with PDF generation functionality in DownloadButton component
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the download button generates and downloads a PDF file instead of a .txt file
The PDF file has a .pdf extension and appropriate MIME type
PDF generation handles all research paper sections without data loss

file_operations.description: Add jsPDF library as a dependency to enable client-side PDF generation functionality

Replace downloadPaper function call with new downloadPaperAsPDF function to generate and download PDF files instead of text files. Update button label and toast messages to reflect PDF download. Ensure all 12 research paper sections are passed to the PDF generation utility

requirements.summary: Format PDF document to match uploaded research paper template structure and styling
acceptanceCriteria: PDF document structure matches the uploaded research paper template format
Section headings, numbering, and hierarchy follow the template conventions
Typography (fonts, sizes, spacing) matches the reference template styling
Page layout and margins are consistent with the template

file_operations.description: Create PDF formatting utility that analyzes the uploaded research paper template (available in assets) to extract formatting conventions including section hierarchy, heading styles, typography (font families, sizes, weights), spacing patterns, and page layout settings. Implement template-matching logic that applies these extracted styles to the generated PDF document

requirements.summary: Update downloadHelper utility to support PDF generation while preserving template-based formatting
acceptanceCriteria: The formatPaper function's template formatting is preserved and applied to PDF output
All 12 research paper sections are correctly rendered in the PDF

file_operations.description: Add downloadPaperAsPDF function that uses jsPDF library to generate PDF documents. Integrate with pdfFormatter utility to apply template-based formatting. Preserve the existing formatPaper function and its logic. Ensure all 12 sections (abstract, introduction, government framework, factors affecting, comparative analysis, objectives, literature review, methodology, data analysis, results, discussion, policy recommendations, conclusion, citations) are correctly formatted and included in the PDF output with proper section numbering and hierarchy
